what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links an image asset to the design-assets channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
standouts: target URI contains a literal space and parentheses: image://design-assets/icons/image (23)
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for a structured metadata record.
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added.
target uses a custom image:// URI scheme.
Filename is derived from the attachment id.
concerns: target URI contains an unescaped space and parentheses, which may require encoding in some consumers.
Asset name image (23) looks auto-generated or placeholder-like.
No additional asset metadata such as size, format, alt text, or checksum is recorded.
notes: A minimal ledger entry binding an image asset URI to the design-assets channel with a creation timestamp.
questions: What resource does image://design-assets/icons/image (23) resolve to?
Is the target URI expected to be percent-encoded when consumed?
Was this attachment generated automatically from a channel message or uploaded manually?
What does the (23) suffix in the asset name signify?
intention_signals: Create a persistent, typed record that associates an image asset with a specific channel for later retrieval or display.
novelty: The target URI uses a custom image:// scheme and includes a space and parentheses in the path, which is uncommon for canonical asset references.
contribution: Provides a concrete attachment binding between the design-assets channel and a specific image asset location, plus a timestamp and type classification that a fused system could use for asset lookup and provenance.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record linking the design-assets channel to the image asset image://design-assets/icons/image (23) with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persistently associate an image asset with the design-assets channel as a typed attachment.
